response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web engineer, but the role's hard requirements are largely unmet: no indication of native-level Japanese proficiency, minimal recent C/UNIX systems programming (brief C/C++ stint in 2014-2015), and no evidence of Linux kernel analysis or modification experience. There is no background in L2/L3 networking, switch products, or security technologies like MACsec or 802.1X, making this a poor fit despite strong general engineering credentials.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Low-latency systems work in DLang (async I/O, fibers) shows systems-programming aptitude
Strong testing/craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD) and knowledge-sharing track record
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — a hard requirement for the role
C/UNIX experience is dated and brief; no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience shown
No exposure to networking security (MACsec, 802.1X), L2/L3 switching, or embedded LAN-switch firmware

key_factors.factor: Lack of C/UNIX and Linux kernel depth
